note: Pinned downstream packages per host, the lockfile of this cross-check. Chosen by download rank on PyPI, not by whether they flatter the result: the point is a sample nobody in this project curated toward an answer. Only the sources are parsed - nothing here is imported or executed.

marshmallow.why: Subclassing IS its documented extension mechanism, so the signal should be present if it exists anywhere.

click.why: A second subclass-style host, to check the marshmallow result is not one package's habit.

flask.why: The control. Flask is extended by REGISTERING against the app object, not by subclassing, so a method that only counts subclasses should find almost nothing here. It does not, and that negative is the useful part.
